payload.title: Task 1515 blocked: no governed shell or file-inspection surface exposed in carrier
payload.summary: Attempted to execute task 1515 inventory for packages/task-lifecycle-kernel, but the current carrier exposes Narada task/inbox/read surfaces without a governed shell executor or local file-inspection tool. EE-MCP doctor reports the old WSL-to-Windows shell adapter is superseded by Windows-native Narada proper and raw Windows shell shortcuts are forbidden for that adapter.
evidence: Task 1515 read-back confirms status claimed for narada.builder and actionable required work: inventory packages/task-lifecycle-kernel before migration.
tool_search for command/shell/filesystem surfaces returned Narada task, inbox, directive, doctrine, checkpoint, and GitHub tools, but no local command runner or filesystem reader.
narada_ee_mcp_doctor returned status superseded_by_windows_native, configured false, raw_windows_shell_forbidden true, and no repair_command.
Because no file inspection path is exposed, the package inventory cannot be performed in this session without an additional governed command/filesystem capability.
proposal: Expose or hydrate a Windows-native governed shell/file-inspection MCP surface for this carrier, then resume task 1515.

After the surface is available, inspect packages/task-lifecycle-kernel for .mjs entrypoints, package scripts/bin surfaces, tests/fixtures, generated artifacts, oversized files, import/export topology, and runtime compatibility constraints.
payload.recommendation: Keep task 1515 claimed but execution-blocked until a governed local inspection capability is available.
